entry.title: Review: Taco Bell - Slow-Roasted Chicken Grande Nachos
entry.summary:
Taco Bell's Slow-Roasted Chicken Nachos Grande features a double portion of slow-roasted chicken (compared to the Cantina Chicken Soft Taco), refried beans, warm nacho cheese sauce, a three-cheese blend (mozzarella, pepper jack, and cheddar), pico de gallo, reduced-fat sour cream, and guacamole, served over a bed of tortilla chips.
It came in a box and cost me $6.99. The box was filled pretty well with a decent amount of chicken, toppings, and chips. Topping coverage was pretty good, although I always find myself wishing for just a little more nacho cheese sauce.
As with a lot of things at Taco Bell, the combination of flavors was very familiar, as the Grande Nachos aren't much different from Taco Bell's previous nachos in a box (or regular menu Nachos BellGrande, for that matter). Compared to Nachos BellGrande, you get more food for a similar price ($5.99 for the seasoned beef version of both at my local Taco Bell). I would guess it to be a third more food. It's definitely shareable. What stood out from the Grande Nachos over Nachos BellGrande was the guacamole and pico de gallo, which added an acidity that the latter lacks (it has sour cream and tomatoes, but they don't add much). The chicken was also interesting, as Taco Bell's limited-time nachos usually come with beef (either seasoned ground beef or steak). The slow-roasted chicken was tender and meaty with a nice seasoning to it. I much prefer it to the chain's previous chicken, which was milder in flavor and less satisfying texture-wise.
Overall, Taco Bell's Slow-Roasted Chicken Grande Nachos get a thumbs up from me. While familiar, there was a nice variety of flavors and textures to go with a good amount of food for the money. Nutritional Info - Taco Bell - Slow-Roasted Chicken Grande Nachos Calories - 1100 Fat - 59g (Saturated Fat - 9g) Sodium - 1720mg Carbs - 111g (Sugar - 6g) Protein - 32g Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Jack in the Box Brings Back the Sourdough Scrambler
entry.summary:
The Sourdough Scrambler makes a limited-time return to Jack in the Box as part of the chain's all-day breakfast menu.
The breakfast sandwich features scrambled eggs, two slices of melted American cheese, and a choice of hickory-smoked bacon strips or a grilled sausage patty on toasted sourdough rounds (the same as you'd get with the Sourdough Jack). You can find my review of the sausage version here. In addition to a la carte, the Sourdough Scrambler is available in a combo with hot coffee and a hash brown. The price on Jack in the Box's Sourdough Scrambler starts at $2.99 (it costs $3.49 near me in Los Angeles). The combo meal costs $5.99 near me. Photo via Jack in the Box. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.summary:
Carl's Jr. is testing the new Really Big Dill Burger for a limited time at participating locations in Central California.
Carl's Jr.'s Really Big Dill Burger (not to be confused with Sonic's Big Dill Cheeseburger) features a charbroiled beef patty, dill aioli sauce, dill pickle fries, pickle crisps, melted Swiss cheese, lettuce, and tomato served on a toasted sesame seed bun. It is also available with two or three beef patties. The Dill pickle fries can also be ordered as a side. Pickles are a trending ingredient/flavor right now, and several fast-food chains have embraced the trend, including Sonic (with a pickle menu), Popeyes (also with a pickle menu), Del Taco (with a fried pickle taco), and more. Photo via Carl's Jr. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Jersey Mike's Getting Rid of Portabella Mushrooms
entry.summary:
Jersey Mike's is in the process of removing portabella mushrooms from its menu. Some locations have already used up the last of their stock, and the mushrooms will no longer be available chain-wide by early October 2025.
The removal of portabella mushrooms affects sandwiches #64 to #66, i.e., the Grilled Portabella Mushroom & Swiss (pictured), the Portabella Cheese Steak, and the Portabella Chicken Cheese Steak. If you're a vegetarian, that leaves The Veggie (#14) as the chain's remaining vegetarian sub. Portabella mushrooms were first offered at Jersey Mike's in March 2021. Photo via Jersey Mike's. entry.title: Culver's Reveals New Jalapeno Cheese Curds and Return of the CurderBurger
entry.summary:
In celebration of its first Curdtoberfest (like Oktoberfest but honoring cheese curds), Culver's reveals new Jalapeno Cheese Curds and the return of the CurderBurger.
Jalapeno Cheese Curds (pictured) will arrive on October 15, 2025 (apparently, the 10th anniversary of National Cheese Curd Day) and will be available for a limited time through October 31, 2025, while supplies last. They're a spicy take on Culver's classic Wisconsin Cheese Curds made with bits of jalapeno pepper and cheddar cheese. The CurderBurger features a Deluxe ButterBurger topped with a breaded and fried patty of cheese (a blend of yellow and white cheddar). It arrives on September 29, 2025, and will be available through October 14, 2025, while supplies last. It first started as an April Fools' joke in 2021, but then they made it into an actual burger, and this is the fifth time they've brought it back. In addition to the limited-time menu items, Culver's first-ever Curdtoberfest will include themed decor at its restaurants and an online sweepstakes with "free Wisconsin Cheese Curds for a year" and other prizes. Photo via Culver's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Moe's Southwest Grill Puts Together New $9.95 Value Meal
entry.summary:
Moe's Southwest Grill debuts the new Moe Value Meal, a discounted combo meal that includes a junior burrito, a side of queso, and a fountain drink for $9.95. As with Moe's other entrees, you also get free chips, salsa, and dipping sauces.
To promote the new offering, Moe's is holding a sweepstakes game, the Moe Value Meal Game Show, with a grand prize of a year of Moe Value Meals, 1,000 prizes of a free Moe Value Meal, and a reward code for 20% off a Moe Value Meal for all participants. You must be a Moe Rewards member to redeem any of the prizes. The game can be found online at www.moevaluemeal.com and runs through October 5, 2025. Photo via Moe's Southwest Grill. entry.title: Carl's Jr. Now Serves Its Chicken Sandwiches on Sourdough Bread
entry.summary:
In an odd bit of a switch, Carl's Jr. now serves almost all of its charbroiled and hand-breaded chicken sandwiches on toasted sourdough bread instead of a bun. The only exception is the value-priced Spicy Chicken Sandwich.
Items now served on sourdough include: - Hand-Breaded Chicken Sandwich (pictured) - Hand-Breaded Bacon Swiss Chicken Sandwich - Charbroiled Chicken Club Sandwich - Charbroiled Santa Fe Chicken Sandwich - Charbroiled BBQ Chicken Sandwich Given that fast food sourdough tends not to be sour, the switch amounts to serving its chicken sandwiches on toast. Photo via Carl's Jr. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.summary:
Crumbl has revealed its limited-time menu for this week, and it includes Hazelnut Sea Salt Cookies and more. The cookie bakery chain celebrates its birthday this week by offering a deal where you can get a 6-pack of cookies for the price of a 4-pack on Wednesday, September 24, 2025.
Here's what you can expect to find on the Crumbl menu from September 22 through September 27, 2025: - Hazelnut Sea Salt Cookie - A warm browned butter cookie stuffed with hazelnut spread and topped with a drizzle of more hazelnut spread and a sprinkle of sea salt. - Confetti Cake - A two-tier confetti cake layered with vanilla buttercream and topped with a sprinkle of rainbow sprinkles. - Birthday Cake Cookie featuring Oreo - A warm cookie made with Oreo cookie pieces, topped with cake batter cream cheese frosting, more Oreo cookie pieces, and rainbow sprinkles. - Original Pink Sugar Cookie - A chilled almond sugar cookie topped with pink almond frosting. - Milk Chocolate Chip Cookie - A warm cookie packed with milk chocolate chips. Additionally, each location has two "secret menu" cookies this week. You can check the Crumbl website or mobile app to see what's being offered near you. Photo via Crumbl. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: McDonald's McWrap is Available at One Location in the US for a Limited Time
entry.summary:
The McWrap has been reintroduced in the US at a single McDonald's location as part of the "Global Favorites" menu at the "global-inspired" restaurant inside McDonald's headquarters in Chicago.
The Global Favorites menu is a rotating menu that pulls McDonald's items from around the world. The current offerings include: - McWrap Classic (Poland) - McCrispy Strips, shredded lettuce, American cheese, mayonnaise, Roma tomatoes, arugula, and Tomato Onion Sauce, wrapped up in a flour tortilla. It's priced at $6.99. - McWrap Sweet Chili (France) - McCrispy Strips, shredded lettuce, American cheese, mayonnaise, Roma tomatoes, and Sweet Chili Sauce, wrapped up in a flour tortilla. It is also priced at $6.99. It's a bit different from the Sweet Chili McWrap offered here in the US over a decade ago. - Big Arch Burger (Global but not in the US... yet) - Two quarter-pound beef patties, three slices of processed white cheddar cheese, crispy-fried onions, slivered onions, pickles, shredded lettuce, and Big Arch sauce on a toasted sesame and poppy seed bun. It's priced at $7.99. - Biscoff McPops (Spain) - Mini donuts filled with Biscoff Cookie Butter and dusted with powdered sugar. It costs $3.99 for three. McPops also appeared on the menu of McDonald's CosMc's concept. - Chocolate Hazelnut McPops (Spain) - Mini donuts with a chocolate hazelnut filling, dusted with powdered sugar. In Spain, they're just called Nutella McPops. They're priced at three for $3.99. - Berry McPops (Switzerland) - Pink mini donuts filled with wild berry jam, dusted with pink powdered sugar. As with the others, it's priced at three for $3.99. The restaurant is open to the public and located at 1035 W Randolph St, Chicago, IL 60607. Photo via McDonald's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: McAlister's Deli Welcomes Back Witch's Brew and Introduces New White Chocolate Chip Pumpkin Pecan Cookie
entry.summary:
McAlister's Deli celebrates the 2025 Halloween season by bringing back its Witch's Brew and launching the new Pumpkin Pecan Cookie with White Chocolate Chips.
McAlister's Witch's Brew is a green-colored lemonade made with cane sugar, lemon juice, and caramel apple syrup. The new Pumpkin Pecan Cookie with White Chocolate Chips features a soft, chewy pumpkin cookie made with warm spices (cinnamon, nutmeg, and ginger), pecans, and white chocolate chips. Both menu items are available for a limited time now through October 31, 2025, at participating McAlister's Deli locations chain-wide, while supplies last. Photo via McAlister's Deli. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Jack in the Box Welcomes 2025 Halloween Menu
entry.summary:
Jack in the Box launches the new Monster Munchies menu for the 2025 Halloween season.
Available now for a limited time, the snackable menu includes several oversized items and more. It includes: - Two Monster Tacos - Two crispy tacos topped with melty American cheese, shredded lettuce, and taco sauce. They're slightly taller and a good deal longer than Jack in the Box's regular tacos. The suggested price starts at $3. - Monster Mozzarella Stick - A 6" breaded and fried mozzarella stick served with two marinara dip cups. Pricing starts at $3. - Monster Churro - A 10" fried churro with a cinnamon sugar cream filling, dusted with cinnamon sugar. As with the tacos and mozzarella stick, pricing starts at $3. - Halloween Monster Cookie Shake - A vanilla shake blended with Chips Ahoy! syrup and Oreo cookie crumbs, topped with whipped topping and more Oreo crumbs. Pricing starts at $4.99. - Monster Cookie Sweet Cream Iced Coffee - For $3.99 (may vary), it's an iced coffee mixed with sweet cream and Chips Ahoy! syrup. In addition to the seasonal menu items, Jack has also put together a Halloween-themed meal: the Monster Munchie Meal. It costs $12 (may vary) and includes: - Two Monster Tacos - A Monster Mozzarella Stick with two marinara dip cups - Four chicken nuggets - Seasoned curly fries - A regular drink - A collectible color-changing Halloween cup Photo via Jack in the Box. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Carl's Jr. Brings Back Spicy Western Bacon Cheeseburger
entry.summary:
The Spicy Western Bacon Cheeseburger is back on the Carl's Jr. menu for a limited time. It takes the Western Bacon Cheeseburger and swaps in pepper jack cheese in place of American cheese and adds sliced jalapenos.
Specifically, the burger features a charbroiled beef patty, two strips of bacon, melted pepper jack cheese, crispy-fried onion rings, tangy BBQ sauce, and pickled jalapeno coins, served on a sesame seed bun. It's also available with two or three beef patties. It should be noted that both pepper jack cheese and pickled jalapeno coins can be found in some regular menu items at Carl's Jr., i.e., you can still custom-order the Spicy Western Bacon Cheeseburger after its limited-time run is over. Photo via Carl's Jr. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: New Dot's Homestyle Pretzels Buffalo Flavored Seasoned Twists Arrive in Stores
entry.summary:
In time for football season, Dot's Homestyle Pretzels introduces a new Buffalo sauce pretzel flavor as a permanent addition to its product line.
The spicy and tangy flavor features "bold Buffalo heat" paired with the brand's "signature buttery crunch." Dot's Homestyle Pretzels Buffalo Flavored Seasoned Twists are available now at retailers nationwide. You can find them in 5-oz, 9.5-oz, 16-oz, and 32-oz bags. Prices vary by retailers, but a 16-oz bag costs $6.99 on the Hershey's online store (Hershey's owns the Dot's Homestyle Pretzel brand). Photo via Dot's Homestyle Pretzels. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Boo! Books Return to Wendy's for Halloween 2025
entry.summary:
Wendy's Boo! Books have made their annual return for Halloween, offering a booklet of five "free Jr. Frosty" coupons for $1.
The booklets also include a "bonus" coupon for a $1.99 Kids' Meal with any purchase. All six coupons can be redeemed through December 31, 2025. 90 cents from the purchase price of each book goes to benefit the Dave Thomas Foundation for Adoption. You can buy the Boo! Books at any participating Wendy's restaurant or through the Wendy's mobile app or website (under the "Give Something Back" category). They can also be ordered in bulk through the Dave Thomas Foundation for Adoption website. Photo via Wendy's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Taco Bell Tests New Double Dipping Burritos
entry.summary:
Taco Bell is testing new Double Dipping Burritos for a limited time at participating locations in the Charlotte, NC area.
The burritos feature a choice of grilled marinated steak or slow-roasted chicken with a melty 3-cheese blend, and Avocado Ranch sauce, wrapped up in a flour tortilla and grilled. You get two snack-sized burritos in an order, and they come with a split dipping cup. The cup is filled with Chili Lime Crema on one size and a crunchy blend of crushed seasoned tortilla chips and crispy cotija cheese. Prices range between $5.99 and $6.99, depending on the location. Taco Bell previously released something similar, Cheesy Dipping Burritos, earlier this year. Photo via Taco Bell. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Outback Serves Up New Platypus-Themed Drinks and More
entry.summary:
Outback Steakhouse welcomes the fall with several featured dishes and a pair of color-changing platypus-themed drinks.
The seasonal menu items include: - Down Under Trio - A shareable platter Bloomin' Onion petals with signature sauce, new Fried Mozzarella Bloomerangs with marinara, and Aussie Cheese Fries with house-made ranch. - Three Cheese Spinach Dip (limited time only) - Made with Mozzarella, Parmesan, cream cheese, and Alfredo sauce. It comes with tortilla chips for dipping. - Filet with Roasted Garlic Butter - A barrel-cut filet topped with roasted garlic butter and paired with a choice of steakhouse potato and a side. - Platypus Punch (pictured; limited time only)- Deep Eddy Grapefruit Vodka, mango, and Country Style Lemonade, served with a color-changing tea egg and a keepsake platypus. - Puggle Splash (limited time only)- A non-alcoholic version of Platypus Punch made with mango lemonade and blue tea. - Kangaroo Court (limited time only)- A Shiraz Cabernet Sauvignon blend created by winemakers Mollydooker. Photo via Outback Steakhouse. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Hungry Howie's Introduces New Detroit-Style Pizza
entry.summary:
Hungry Howie's honors its Detroit roots (the pizza chain was founded in the Detroit suburbs in 1973) with the launch of its new Detroit-Style Pizza.
The new pizza features a rectangular pizza with a "thick, airy crust, crispy caramelized edges, cheese layered to the edge, and sauce added as the final topping." Hungry Howie's Detroit-Style Pizza is available now at participating locations chain-wide. Each pizza includes eight slices, and prices start at $12.99 (it's $14.99 here in Los Angeles). Photo via Hungry Howie's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: White Chocolate Crunch Bars are Back
entry.summary:
The white chocolate version of a Crunch bar, Crunch White, is back in stores for a limited time after a 20-year hiatus.
Where the Crunch bar features a milk chocolate candy bar with crisped rice, the Crunch White replaces the milk chocolate with white chocolate. The limited-time candy bar arrives in time for the 2025 Halloween season and can be found in 1.55-oz single-size bars as well as 2.7-oz Share packs. Crunch White bars are available now through December 2025 at retailers nationwide. Photo via Ferrero. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Review: Pizza Hut - Chicken Bacon Ranch Crafted Flatzz
entry.summary:
Pizza Hut's Chicken Bacon Ranch Crafted Flatzz is a "hand-crafted" personal pizza topped with crispy-fried boneless chicken pieces, applewood-smoked bacon, Ultimate Ranch sauce, mozzarella, and a parmesan oregano seasoning.
I got one for $5 during lunch time (the price goes up to $6.99 after 5PM). I also reviewed the Nashville Hot Chicken one earlier.
The Crafted Flatzz is about twice the size of a Pizza Hut personal pan pizza, but a good deal thinner (and less greasy) with a hand-stretched oval crust. It was somewhat football-shaped, which is fitting for the season, I suppose. It was very crispy on the surface while maintaining a soft crumb inside. The pizza was cut into eight two-bite (or one big bite) slices with one small popcorn chicken-esque crispy chicken piece per slice.
The mozzarella was nicely melted, but the underlying Ultimate Ranch just tasted like your run-of-the-mill ranch to me. Between the two, there was a moderate creaminess and a herby tang throughout the pizza. The parmesan oregano didn't make much of an impact. The bacon was a little sparse here, and I could have used a little more to cut against the strong ranch flavor. I feel like it's one of those "your mileage may vary" things, though.
Overall, Pizza Hut's Chicken Bacon Ranch Crafted Flatzz is nice for the price and crust, but leans too heavily on ranch as its primary flavor driver. Typically, when something is called "chicken bacon ranch," it's mostly chicken, then bacon, then ranch. This was more like "ranch chicken bacon." I would definitely choose the Nashville Hot Chicken Crafted Flatzz over it. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Papa Johns Debuts New Papa Dippa
entry.summary:
Papa Johns introduces the new Papa Dippa, which the pizza chain is calling the "first-ever pizza designed exclusively for dippin'. It's joined by three new dipping sauces: Roasted Parmesan, Garlic Hot Honey, and Creamy Garlic Pesto.
The Papa Dippa is a large, three-cheese (provolone, Fontina, and Asiago) pepperoni pizza with a flattened edge cut into 16 strips/sticks instead of slices. It comes with a "Garlic Sauce Flight," which includes the three new sauces as well as the chain's signature Special Garlic Sauce. Here's how Papa Johns describes the new dipping sauces:
Roasted Garlic Parmesan - "A Caesar-style blend with roasted garlic and Parmesan cheese." Garlic Hot Honey - "Sweet heat with garlic and chili flakes for an added kick.​" Creamy Garlic Pesto - "Smooth yet textured with basil and Parmesan for a rich, herbaceous dip."
The Papa Dippa is available now for a limited time nationwide for $13.99 (may vary; it's $14.99 at my local Papa Johns here in Los Angeles). Nutritional Info - Papa Johns Papa Dippa Serving Size - 1 stick Calories - 160 Fat - 6g (Saturated Fat - 3g) Sodium - 340mg Carbs - 18g (Sugar - 2g) Protein - 6g Photo via Papa Johns. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Taco Bell Launches New Orange Vanilla Cream Soda Freeze
entry.summary:
Taco Bell updates its Freeze menu with the addition of the new Orange Vanilla Cream Soda Freeze, as well as a non-orange-flavored version, the Vanilla Cream Soda Freeze.
The Orange Vanilla Cream Soda Freeze features a sweet vanilla frozen, carbonated slushy base swirled with sweet vanilla creamer and tangy orange syrup. Pricing starts at $3.69 for a regular, $3.89 for a large. Regular Freezes of any flavor are also discounted to $1 during the chain's daily Happier Hour from 2 to 5PM. The Vanilla Cream Soda Freeze is the same as the Orange Vanilla Cream Soda Freeze, minus the orange-flavored syrup. Pricing is the same as well. Photo via Taco Bell. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: New Popeyes x Hot Ones Menu Arrives
entry.summary:
Popeyes partners with popular YouTube series Hot Ones to debut the new Popeyes x Hot Ones menu, featuring Hot Ones-inspired dishes with varying degrees of heat.
Available for a limited time at participating locations nationwide, the Popeyes x Hot Ones menu includes:
Sizzlin' Sriracha Dippers (Mild) - Pickled garlic sriracha-marinated Chicken Dippers, served with a Buttermilk Ranch dip cup and a Hot Ones Sriracha sachet. Smokin' Rojo Sandwich (Medium)- A crispy-fried chicken filet topped with Hot Ones' Los Calientes Rojo spread and pickles, on a toasted brioche bun. Darin' Dab Ghost Wings (Hot) - Your choice of bone-in or boneless wings dusted with a Ghost Pepper dry rub and served with a Hot Ones Last Dab Ranch dip cup. The Last Dab (Extremely Hot) - A sachet of Hot Ones' spiciest, signature hot sauce. It's available a la carte for $1.
For those who want to do their own Hot Ones-type challenge, Popeyes has put together the Popeyes x Hot Ones bundle. It includes the Sizzlin' Sriracha Dippers, a Sriracha sachet, a Smokin' Rojo Sandwich, and Darin' Dab Ghost Wings (bone-in or boneless) with Last Dab Ranch for $16.99. As part of the collaboration, the Popeyes x Hot Ones menu will be featured on an episode of the show, making Popeyes the first wing brand to be spotlighted on the show. Photo via Popeyes. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Taco Bell Testing Two New Energy Refresca Flavors
entry.summary:
Taco Bell looks into expanding its Energy Refresca lineup with a test of the new Electric Punch Energy Refresca and Berry Burst Energy Refresca for a limited time at participating locations in the San Francisco Bay area.
The Electric Punch Energy Refresca features a tropical punch-flavored, fizzy drink with green coffee extract, L-theanine, vitamin B12, and guarana for energy. It contains 160mg of caffeine. The Berry Burst Energy Refresca is a berry-flavored, carbonated drink infused with green coffee extract, L-theanine, vitamin B12, and guarana. It contains 160mg of caffeine. Both drinks are available for a suggested price of $4.99. Taco Bell currently serves the Tropical Punch Rockstar Energy Refresca and the Pineapple Lime Rockstar Energy Refresca nationwide for the same suggested price. Photo via Taco Bell. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Taco John's Introduces New Jr. Crispy Chicken Wraps
entry.summary:
Taco John's serves up new Jr. Crispy Chicken Wraps for $1.79 each. Each wrap features a familiar combination of a crispy-fried white-meat chicken tender, shredded lettuce, a shredded four-cheese blend, and ranch, rolled up in a warm flour tortilla.
Regular and spicy versions are available, with the only difference between the two being the addition of pickled jalapenos in the spicy variety. For a limited time, Jr. Crispy Chicken Wraps are also available as part of a two-for-$3 mix-and-match deal. You can also get them as part of the $5 Jr. Crispy Chicken Wrap Meal, which includes two of the wraps and Jr. Potato Olés. In addition to the new snackable wraps, Taco John's has made several of its Meal Steals available for in-person ordering (the value combos were previously only available when ordering online). The following Meal Steals are now available to order in person as well as digitally:
$5 Meal Steal - A Crispy Beef Taco, a Bean Burrito, Jr. Potato Olés, and a small fountain drink. $7 Meal Steal - Two Crispy Beef Tacos, a Nacho Crunch Beef Burrito, Jr. Potato Olés, and a small fountain drink. $9 Meal Steal - A Super Crispy Beef Taco, a Chicken Stuffed Grilled Taco, Jr. Potato Olés, a Churro, and a small fountain drink.
The $7 Burrito Bundle and $7 Taco Takeover remain as online-only specials. Photo via Taco John's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Never Ending Pasta Bowl is Back at Olive Garden
entry.summary:
The all-you-can-eat Never Ending Pasta Bowl promotion is back at Olive Garden for a limited time through Sunday, November 16, 2025.
Starting at $13.99, you can choose from four pastas, six sauces, and three all-you-can-eat toppings (for an additional $4.99. The Never Ending Pasta Bowl selection includes:
Pastas - Angel Hair, Fettuccine, Rigatoni, and Spaghetti Sauces - Alfredo, Five Cheese Marinara, Meat Sauce, and Spicy Three-Meat Sauce. Toppings - Crispy Chicken Fritta, Meatballs, and Italian Sausage
The Spicy Three-Meat Sauce is new to the promotion. It's also fairly new to Olive Garden, as it was introduced earlier this year. It's a meat sauce flavored with cherry peppers, chili flakes, garlic, herbs, pepperoni, red bell pepper, and red wine. Your meal also includes unlimited soup or salad, plus unlimited breadsticks. Tips for making the most of the promotion include asking for smaller portions to sample the various offerings and asking them to prep your next bowl as you're working on your current one, as there can be a bit of a wait otherwise. Photo via Olive Garden. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Taco Bell Testing New Cantina Chicken Double Tostada
entry.summary:
Taco Bell is testing the new Cantina Chicken Double Tostada for a limited time at participating locations in the Austin, TX area.
Resembling Taco Bell's Mexican Pizza, the new test item features a layer of slow-roasted chicken and black beans sandwiched by two crunchy tostada shells, topped with Avocado Verde sauce, a melted three-cheese blend (pepper jack, mozzarella, and cheddar), reduced-fat sour cream, purple cabbage, and pico de gallo. As with other Cantina Chicken menu items, a packet of Avocado Verde Salsa is included on the side. The Cantina Chicken Double Tostada is priced at $6.99. It's also available in a combo that includes a Cantina Chicken Double Tostada, a Soft Taco, and an Aqua Refresca for $10.49. Photo via Taco Bell. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Fresh Fit Menu Returns to Subway
entry.summary:
Subway brings back its better-for-you Fresh Fit menu nationwide on September 17, 2025 (some locations are already offering the menu).
Each sandwich on the Fresh Fit menu features 20g or more protein, a full serving of veggies, and less than 500 calories in a six-inch sub. They include: - Grilled Chicken & Avocado (35g protein) - Grilled chicken, smashed avocado, lettuce, spinach, tomatoes, cucumbers, red onions, and smoky Baja Chipotle sauce on multigrain bread. - Ham & Turkey Stacker (20g protein) - Oven-roasted turkey and Black Forest ham, lettuce, spinach, tomatoes, cucumbers, red onions, and yellow mustard on multigrain bread. - Seasoned Steak & Avocado (35g protein) - Seasoned steak, smashed avocado, lettuce, spinach, tomatoes, and red onions on multigrain bread. - Turkey & Ranch Delite (26g protein) - Oven-roasted turkey, lettuce, spinach, tomatoes, red onions, and Peppercorn Ranch on multigrain bread. According to Subway, the new menu "returns with more flavor and options to fuel today's active lifestyles." Photo via Subway. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Krispy Kreme Launches New Fall Fair Donuts
entry.summary:
Krispy Kreme turns to fairs for inspiration in launching its new Fall Fair Collection of seasonal donuts.
Available for a limited time at participating locations chain-wide, the collection includes four donuts: - Cotton Candy Donut - An Original Glazed donut dipped in blue icing and cotton candy-flavored sugar, then topped with a strawberry-flavored icing swirl and a dollop of cotton candy-flavored buttercreme. - Kreme Puff Donut - A ring donut topped with a swirl of Kreme, a Donut Dot (donut hole), and a dusting of sweet powdered coating. - Blue Ribbon Apple Pie Donut - A shell donut with apple cinnamon filling, dipped in caramel-flavored icing, sprinkled with cinnamon cookie crumbs, and decorated with a white icing cross-hatch. - Caramel Churro Donut - A ring donut covered in cinnamon sugar and drizzled with caramel-flavored icing. Krispy Kreme's Fall Fair Collection can be purchased individually and by the dozen. Photo via Krispy Kreme. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Panera Brings Back Ciabatta Dippers
entry.summary:
Ciabatta Dippers are back on the menu at Panera for a limited time nationwide, and are available in two varieties: the returning French Dipper and the new Chicken Pesto Dipper.
The melty sandwiches come with a portion of soup for dipping. The French Dipper features marinated steak, provolone, asiago, and garlic aioli on ciabatta, served with Bistro French Onion Soup. The new Chicken Pesto Dipper slots in for the previous Spicy Chicken Dipper and includes grilled chicken, provolone, parmesan, and basil pesto on ciabatta, paired with Creamy Tomato Soup. Prices vary, but the French Dipper costs $13.69 near me, while the Chicken Pesto Dipper is $11.99. Photo via Panera. Read more at Brand Eating!
